name: Rheumatoid Arthritis - DMARD-Only Patients (No Diagnosis)
description: Tricky test case: finds patients on DMARDs/biologics who have NO formal rheumatoid arthritis diagnosis on their problem list (Path C). DMARDs are commonly prescribed for non-RA indications: methotrexate (psoriasis, ectopic pregnancy, malignancy), hydroxychloroquine (lupus, malaria, COVID-era empirical use), sulfasalazine (inflammatory bowel disease, ankylosing spondylitis), TNF inhibitors (Crohn's, psoriasis, ankylosing spondylitis). This makes Path C particularly important to evaluate — an LLM that queries only Condition for RA will overcount RA prevalence and miss the cross-indication noise.
prompt: I want to find patients in our system who are taking DMARDs such as methotrexate, hydroxychloroquine, or anti-TNF biologics but don't have a formal rheumatoid arthritis diagnosis on their problem list. These medications are commonly prescribed for many other indications including lupus, psoriasis, IBD, and ankylosing spondylitis.

prompts.naive: Find patients on DMARDs without a rheumatoid arthritis diagnosis.
prompts.expert: I need to identify the documentation gap that is especially common with DMARDs because of cross-indication prescribing: patients on methotrexate, hydroxychloroquine, sulfasalazine, adalimumab, or infliximab who lack a formal RA Condition resource. Two-step approach: first find all patients with MedicationRequest for DMARD ingredients (RxNorm 6851, 5521, 9524, 327361, 191831), then exclude those with a Condition for RA subtypes (69896004, 11055151000119108, 201766009, 1073811000119102, 143441000119108).

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Path C 'tricky' patients: have DMARD/biologic meds but NO RA Condition resource. The Synthea module weights this path moderately (~30%) to reflect cross-indication DMARD prescribing.
